(2) A agrees to work for P’s benefit, and
(3) A agrees to work subject to P’s control

rule_candidates.normalized_text:
Estoppel
(1) P makes false representation or conceals material fact
(2) With intent to create third party’s reliance
(3) third party relies, and
(4) Reliance reasonably based on P’s false representation
rule_candidates.raw_text:
Estoppel
(1) P makes false representation or conceals material fact
(2) With intent to create 3P’s reliance
(3) 3P relies, and
(4) Reliance rxly based on P’s false representation

PRINCIPAL’S LIABILITY

Partially Disclosed Principal
•    3P knows A is working for P but does not know P’s identity
•    3P can sue P or A

Disclosed Principal
•    3P knows A is working for P and P’s identity
•    3P can only sue P

Undisclosed Principal
•  3P does not know A is working for P or P’s identity
•  3P can only sue A
AGENT’S LIABILITY
UNDERLYING CAUSE OF ACTION: TORT CLAIM

rule_candidates.normalized_text:
Vicarious Liability
•
P liable for employee’s torts committed within scope of employment
•
Generally, not liable for independent contractor’s torts
rule_candidates.raw_text:
Vicarious Liability
•
P liable for employee’s torts committed within scope of employment
•
Generally, not liable for independent contractor’s torts

rule_candidates.normalized_text:
Scope of Employment
(1) Conduct occurred within general limitations of employment
(2) A motivated to act for P’s benefit
(3) Act within A’s assigned duties

Intentional Torts
•

rule_candidates.normalized_text:
Commuting for business is within scope of employment
Direct Liability
•
P directly liable to third party if
(i) Consented or approved of A’s tortious conduct toward third party
(ii) Negligently hired or managed A, or
(iii) Assigned a non-delegable duty

(vii) Indemnity for A’s own torts
